title: Prescription Freedom Act of 2025
plain_summary: This bill would stop the Food and Drug Administration from deciding which medicines need a doctor's prescription. Instead, each state would get to make those rules for themselves. The change would happen six months after the bill becomes law. However, the FDA would still control prescription rules for drugs that end pregnancies. This means states could allow people to buy many medicines without seeing a doctor first.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Energy and Commerce.
subjects: Drug safety, medical device, and laboratory regulation

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Prescription Freedom Act of two thousand twenty five.

sections.title: Section 2  Repeal of FDA Authority to Require Prescriptions
sections.text: Starting six months after this law is passed, the Food and Drug Administration will no longer have the power to decide that certain drugs can only be sold with a doctor's prescription. This change removes a specific part of federal law that currently gives the FDA this authority. After this change takes effect, any federal law or rule that talks about prescription drugs will instead follow whatever rules each state has about prescriptions. Federal officials will decide how to match up the old federal rules with each state's rules when needed. However, the Secretary of Health and Human Services can still keep the old prescription requirements for any drugs that are meant to end pregnancies.
